rag_summary.HEA alloy Ni33.9864Cr13.9944Co17.9928Al1: The composition Al16.38525-Co16.38525-Cr16.38525-Fe16.38525-Ni34.4089-Y0.05, processed via arc-melting with Y doping and suction casting, forms a eutectic lamellar structure with γ' (L12) and β (B2) phases due to rapid cooling promoting coupled growth. Y segregation at the scale/metal interface, enabled by Y doping during oxidation, promotes inward oxygen diffusion favoring continuous Al₂O₃ scale formation and prevents interfacial pore formation via vacancy annihilation, thereby suppressing scale spallation. This continuous Al₂O₃ scale inhibits oxygen penetration, providing excellent oxidation resistance at 1000-1100°C. Concurrently, the Al-rich β phase acts as an Al reservoir for continuous Al₂O₃ formation, while the low-energy lamellar structure resists thermal degradation, retaining mechanical properties after oxidation. In contrast, the composition Al16.39344-Co16.39344-Cr16.39344-Fe16.39344-Ni34.42623, processed via vacuum arc melting and machining/polishing, forms a layered biphasic eutectic structure (FCC + B2 phases). The Al-Ni-rich B2 phase, with higher dislocation density, and the electrochemical potential difference between FCC and B2 phases drive pitting corrosion. The passive film, dominated by Cr₂O₃ and Al₂O₃ oxides, exhibits temperature-dependent stability due to decreasing Cr₂O₃ content with temperature. The eutectic lamellar structure (FCC-ordered L12 and BCC-ordered B2 phases) leads to poor oxide scale adhesion with interfacial pores due to differential diffusion rates creating vacancies that coalesce, and spinel formation at the oxide scale surface because spherical/cubic β-phase regions reduce effectiveness for exclusive Al₂O₃ formation, despite the Al-rich B2 phase providing sufficient aluminum for Al₂O₃ formation. The composition Al16.67-Co16.67-Cr16.67-Fe16.67-Ni33.32, processed via arc melting with four remelts and heat treatment at 1200°C followed by water quenching, exhibits 81.7% FCC phase fraction due to high negative mixing enthalpy of Al-Ni, limiting continuous Al₂O₃ formation and leading to Cr₂O₃ spallation, resulting in a specific weight change of -0.07 mg/cm² after 10 cycles. The Al₂O₃ and Cr₂O₃ oxide layers with spinel oxides cause reduced hardness near the surface due to Al depletion and an oxidation rate constant (Kp ~10⁻⁹ g²cm⁻⁴s⁻¹) from partial Al₂O₃ layer formation. The composition Al23.08-Co15.38-Cr15.38-Fe15.38-Ni30.78, processed via arc melting with four flips and heat treatment at 1200°C followed by water quenching, achieves 83.5% BCC/B2 phase fraction, providing sufficient Al for a continuous thin Al₂O₃ oxide layer. This layer limits oxide scale thickening to 9 μm, prevents spalling during thermal cycling (minimal specific weight change of 0.36 mg/cm²), and reduces oxidation rate to the lowest constant (Kp=5.5×10⁻⁹ g²cm⁻⁴s⁻¹) by blocking oxygen ingress. Conversely, the composition Al15.38-Co15.38-Cr23.08-Fe15.38-Ni30.78, processed similarly, forms 72.1% FCC phase + 27.9% BCC/B2 phase, promoting selective Cr₂O₃-rich oxide layer formation due to higher Cr content. This layer spalls during thermal cycling due to CTE mismatch and volatilization, causing a specific weight change of 0.29 mg/cm² and a higher oxidation rate constant (Kp ~1.5×10⁻⁸ g²cm⁻⁴s⁻¹) from exposing fresh metal surfaces. The composition Al21.43-Co14.28-Cr21.43-Fe14.28-Ni28.58, processed via arc melting with four remelts and heat treatment, achieves 99.9% BCC/B2 phase fraction, enabling a 16 μm thick oxide layer containing Al₂O₃ and Cr₂O₃. This layer maintains adhesion during cycling (0.56 mg/cm² weight change) and reduces oxygen ingress despite Cr volatility, yielding an oxidation rate constant (Kp) of 1.5×10⁻⁸ g²cm⁻⁴s⁻¹, though BCC phase consumption creates an FCC-rich subsurface layer, reducing hardness. The composition Al9.1-Co27.3-Cr18.2-Fe18.2-Ni27.3-Ti0.0, designed with Al substitution for Ti and aged at 900°C, forms continuous Cr₂O₃ oxide layers due to increased Cr activity, providing oxidation resistance at 900°C by hindering oxygen diffusion, but β phase precipitates during aging reduce mechanical properties. The composition Al7.85-Co20.56-Cr12.23-Fe11.48-Hf0.0-Mo0.0-Ni40.73-Ta0.0-Ti7.15-W0.0, processed via directional solidification and aging, forms γ' precipitates (50-70% volume fraction) but exhibits higher oxidation weight gain at 1100°C due to complex oxides (Ni,Co)O, CoFe₂O₄, Fe₃Ti₃O₁₀, TiO₂ and γ' depletion reducing Al availability, limiting continuous Al₂O₃ formation.
